D2 Vision L1 Remember Atom

Anatomy of the Eye

tour the optical parts that bend light onto the retina, structure by structure

Light passes the cornea, the pupil set by the iris, and the crystalline lens before forming an image on the retina — sharpest at the fovea, absent at the optic disc.

Step the selector through each structure. The highlighted part is named with its role, and a light ray shows how the cornea and lens converge it onto the fovea.
